message:
data+test: sync tracker, extend raptorq invariants with WASM provenance + status snapshot coverage

Beads tracker (.beads/issues.jsonl):
- Sync with latest concurrent agent state including E5 rejected-tuning-candidate
  sentinel completion and transport stream waiter review updates.

Audit index (audit_index.jsonl):
- Append 9 new deep-audit index entries covering websocket handshake, happy
  eyeballs, transport, gRPC, bytes, codec, and fs subsystem reviews.

RaptorQ perf invariants (tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs):
- Add WASM browser provenance attestation validation: verify
  wasm_browser_provenance_attestation_v1.json schema, field completeness,
  and cross-links to the artifact integrity manifest and SBOM.
- Add WASM browser SBOM validation: verify wasm_browser_sbom_v1.json schema
  completeness and dependency graph structure.
- Add artifact integrity manifest cross-link coverage for the WASM build
  reproducibility chain.

RaptorQ expected-loss decision contract docs:
- Extend docs/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract.md with WASM provenance
  attestation cross-references and status snapshot contract field documentation.

Track-F wavefront pipeline artifact:
- Refresh pipeline stage state with current wavefront positions.

WASM browser artifacts:
- Update integrity manifest, provenance attestation, and SBOM with current
  build metadata and dependency versions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-36m6p.20","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Lock override rejected-candidate provenance to explicit none sentinel","description":"Bounded E5 support slice. Current override-truthfulness contracts already scrub canonical tuning candidate ids, rationale strings, and comparator deltas for runtime-override-unbacked runs, but the external bench/E2E/docs contract does not yet pin the rejected-tuning-candidate surface to an explicit override-safe sentinel.

Planned scope:
- tighten the live override contract so runtime-override-unbacked runs preserve `rejected_tuning_candidate_ids = none` truthfully instead of leaving this implicit;
- update the baseline/profile docs and E2E validator to require the same sentinel;
- add narrow regression coverage in `src/raptorq/gf256.rs` without touching the already-dirty shared `tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs` surface.

Validation:
- `bash -n scripts/run_raptorq_e2e.sh`
- targeted `rch exec` cargo test selector for the new/updated gf256 regression(s)
- targeted `rch exec` cargo check for the touched surfaces' owning crate
- `git diff --check` on touched files","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:17.713664917Z","created_by":"TanBass","updated_at":"2026-04-07T16:12:04.940555672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T16:12:04.940206384Z","close_reason":"Completed in commit 69fd777e; manual-env-override rejected_tuning_candidate_ids none sentinel is now enforced in docs and E2E validation, with parent-bead completion recorded by TanBass on 2026-04-07.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.20","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:17.713664917Z","created_by":"TanBass","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-36m6p.21","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Lock override rejected-candidate provenance to explicit none sentinel","description":"Bounded E5 support slice. Current override truthfulness contracts already scrub canonical tuning candidate ids, rationale strings, and comparator deltas for runtime override unbacked runs, but the external bench, E2E, and docs contract does not yet pin the rejected tuning candidate surface to an explicit override safe sentinel.

Planned scope:
- tighten the live override contract so runtime override unbacked runs preserve rejected_tuning_candidate_ids = none truthfully instead of leaving this implicit
- update the baseline profile docs and E2E validator to require the same sentinel
- add narrow regression coverage in src/raptorq/gf256.rs without touching the already dirty shared tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs surface

Validation:
- bash -n scripts/run_raptorq_e2e.sh
- targeted rch exec cargo test selector for the new or updated gf256 regressions
- targeted rch exec cargo check for the touched surfaces owning crate
- git diff --check on touched files","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:48.272517959Z","created_by":"TanBass","updated_at":"2026-04-07T16:12:35.317275284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T16:12:35.316776247Z","close_reason":"Duplicate child created for the same TanBass E5 none-sentinel slice already completed via asupersync-36m6p.20 and landed on main in commit 69fd777e.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.21","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:48.272517959Z","created_by":"TanBass","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-36m6p.22","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Publish current-head raw-sample multiscenario successor packet","description":"Background:
The checked-in broader E5 guardrail is still `artifacts/raptorq_track_e_gf256_multiscenario_refresh_v3.json`, but the latest 2026-04-07 E5 comments report a current-head raw-sample refresh whose tail story differs materially from v3 and requires a new versioned packet rather than mutating v3 in place.

Technical approach:
- Re-run the current-head RQ-E-GF256-DUAL corpus with rch-offloaded benchmark capture only.
- Build a new versioned artifact packet from the raw-sample/criterion outputs, keeping v2/v3 historical.
- Update the Track-E decision docs/tests so the current broader packet, closure state, and blocker wording stay truthful and machine-checkable.
- Keep scope bounded to artifact/doc/test surfaces plus any minimal helper updates needed to support the capture.

Validation plan:
- Use only `rch exec -- ...` for cargo/bench/test work.
- Re-run the targeted `tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs` guards that pin the broader E5 packet linkage and blocker semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A new versioned broader Track-E artifact is checked in with current-head raw-sample or criterion-backed evidence and an explicit closure assessment.
2. `docs/raptorq_optimization_decision_records.md`, linked JSON artifacts, and `tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs` all point at the new current packet truthfully.
3. Historical packets remain versioned and historical rather than silently rewritten.
4. All cargo-heavy validation for this bead runs through `rch exec -- ...`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyMill","created_at":"2026-04-08T02:00:39.216342684Z","created_by":"RainyMill","updated_at":"2026-04-08T02:31:00.971978595Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T02:31:00.971642431Z","close_reason":"Completed: published v4 raw-sample successor packet, updated linked docs/artifacts/tests, and validated via rch-backed check/test lanes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","gf256","performance","raptorq","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.22","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-08T02:00:39.216342684Z","created_by":"RainyMill","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3087,"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.22","author":"RainyMill","text":"RainyMill claimed this bounded E5 support slice after `br ready --json` returned empty and `bv --robot-next`/frontier inspection showed the real blocker is stale checked-in broader evidence around `artifacts/raptorq_track_e_gf256_multiscenario_refresh_v3.json` versus the newer 2026-04-07 raw-sample capture comments on `asupersync-36m6p`.

Current reserved write surface (Agent Mail reason `asupersync-36m6p.22`):
- `artifacts/raptorq_track_e_gf256_multiscenario_refresh_v4.json`
- `artifacts/raptorq_optimization_decision_records_v1.json`
- `artifacts/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract_v1.json`
- `docs/raptorq_baseline_bench_profile.md`
- `docs/raptorq_optimization_decision_records.md`
- `docs/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract.md`
- `tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs`

Planned execution:
1. Re-run the current-head `RQ-E-GF256-DUAL` broader corpus via `rch exec -- ...` only.
2. Extract the current-head broader evidence into a new versioned successor packet instead of mutating v3.
3. Update docs/tests/artifacts so the live blocker semantics remain machine-checkable.

I am intentionally avoiding `src/raptorq/gf256.rs` and other implementation surfaces unless a minimal capture-support fix becomes necessary.","created_at":"2026-04-08T02:01:51Z"},{"id":3088,"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.22","author":"ubuntu","text":"RainyMill progress update: published the bounded v4 successor packet plus linked decision/docs/test updates. Agent Mail is still timing out / transport-failing on my side, so bead comments remain the durable coordination path. Validation is running strictly through rch: cargo fmt --check has already reported unrelated pre-existing drift outside this slice in src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs, src/transport/router.rs, and src/web/session.rs; cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test -p asupersync --test raptorq_perf_invariants are still in flight.","created_at":"2026-04-08T02:19:09Z"},{"id":3089,"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.22","author":"ubuntu","text":"RainyMill validation update: the v4 packet-linked test target (cargo test -p asupersync --test raptorq_perf_invariants -- --nocapture) is green through rch, and cargo check --all-targets is green through a clean remote rerun. cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs, src/transport/router.rs, and src/web/session.rs. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings is still actively running on rch worker vmi1153651 with no diagnostics emitted yet.","created_at":"2026-04-08T02:26:19Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-36m6p.23","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Reuse shared mul setup for split-biased dual-mul sequential default","description":"Current x86 Track-E defaults are intentionally split-biased for dual-mul (`mul_min_total > mul_max_total` keeps `mul_decision = Sequential`), but `gf256_mul_slices2()` currently satisfies that path by calling the single-lane mul kernel twice and repeating setup work. Take a bounded E5 support slice on `src/raptorq/gf256.rs` to preserve the public decision/log contract while reusing shared mul setup for the sequential dual-mul default where safe. Add focused regression coverage proving the new split-biased helper still matches two independent single-lane mul paths, and validate only with `rch exec -- ...` cargo lanes plus a targeted dual-policy benchmark/check lane that reports the next honest frontier.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldenMoose","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:12.421172164Z","created_by":"GoldenMoose","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:12.421172164Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","gf256","performance","raptorq","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.23","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:12.421172164Z","created_by":"GoldenMoose","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-36m6p.23","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Reuse shared mul setup for split-biased dual-mul sequential default","description":"Current x86 Track-E defaults are intentionally split-biased for dual-mul (`mul_min_total > mul_max_total` keeps `mul_decision = Sequential`), but `gf256_mul_slices2()` currently satisfies that path by calling the single-lane mul kernel twice and repeating setup work. Take a bounded E5 support slice on `src/raptorq/gf256.rs` to preserve the public decision/log contract while reusing shared mul setup for the sequential dual-mul default where safe. Add focused regression coverage proving the new split-biased helper still matches two independent single-lane mul paths, and validate only with `rch exec -- ...` cargo lanes plus a targeted dual-policy benchmark/check lane that reports the next honest frontier.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverBadger","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:12.421172164Z","created_by":"GoldenMoose","updated_at":"2026-04-10T21:53:22.274488183Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T21:53:22.274004725Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","gf256","performance","raptorq","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.23","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:12.421172164Z","created_by":"GoldenMoose","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3143,"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.23","author":"SilverBadger","text":"2026-04-10 SilverBadger: taking over this E5 leaf after fresh bv triage. GoldenMoose is inactive in Agent Mail (~19h last_active), there are no active file reservations on src/raptorq/gf256.rs, and Track-E remains the top actionable blocker for Track-G/E3. Scope stays bounded to src/raptorq/gf256.rs on the split-biased dual-mul sequential-default helper path only; validation will use rch-offloaded targeted cargo lanes plus an honest next-frontier cargo check/clippy/fmt report.","created_at":"2026-04-10T21:38:41Z"},{"id":3144,"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.23","author":"SilverBadger","text":"2026-04-10 SilverBadger: completed the bounded E5 kernel slice in src/raptorq/gf256.rs. Change: gf256_mul_slices2() now preserves the split-biased sequential dual-mul decision contract while reusing shared per-scalar mul setup across both lanes instead of rebuilding setup twice through two independent mul_slice dispatches. Added focused regression test mul_slices2_sequential_shared_setup_matches_two_independent_mul_slice_calls to prove the new sequential shared-setup helper remains bit-exact against two independent single-lane mul paths. Validation via rch: PASS cargo test -p asupersync --lib raptorq::gf256::tests::mul_slices2_sequential_shared_setup_matches_two_independent_mul_slice_calls -- --exact --nocapture; PASS cargo check --all-targets; PASS cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; PASS cargo check --bench raptorq_benchmark --features simd-intrinsics. Full cargo fmt --check remains red only on unrelated pre-existing workspace drift (e.g. src/cancel/symbol_cancel.rs, src/channel/watch.rs, src/combinator/hedge.rs, src/runtime/reactor/token.rs, src/sync/semaphore.rs, src/web/response.rs).","created_at":"2026-04-10T21:53:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-36m6p.3","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Re-establish clean remote cargo check all-targets evidence","description":"Run cargo check --all-targets remotely via rch with a fresh dedicated target dir and record a valid result for E5 without any local fail-open fallback. If the command fails remotely, capture the concrete frontier and stop at evidence unless a narrow non-overlapping fix surface is obvious.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeGrove","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-06T00:47:52.798075425Z","created_by":"JadeGrove","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:20.667161577Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:20.667161577Z","close_reason":"Remote rch cargo check --all-targets produced valid compiler frontier at tests/stream_sink_integration.rs:300 (poll_peek now requires pinning); evidence captured and handed to current owner","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:20.667137663Z","deleted_by":"system","delete_reason":"merge deletion","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","gf256","raptorq","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T00:47:52.798075425Z","created_by":"JadeGrove","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2384,"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.3","author":"JadeGrove","text":"[2026-03-05 JadeGrove] Remote-only validation run for clean E5 cargo-check evidence executed via rch: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-jadeg-e5-check-alltargets cargo check --all-targets. Result: valid remote compiler failure, not local fail-open. Frontier: tests/stream_sink_integration.rs:300, E0599 no method named poll_peek on Peekable in current scope; compiler suggests pinning the stream expression before calling poll_peek. This means the prior invalidated cargo check line is now replaced by concrete blocker evidence. I am not editing this surface because GoldTower announced ownership of the current stream/repro check-clippy frontier.","created_at":"2026-03-06T00:52:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-36m6p.4","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Clear current lib clippy frontier in stream fuse combinator","description":"Confirm and, if still present, fix the current lib-level clippy frontier in src/stream/fuse.rs so broader Track E gate evidence can progress without overlapping reserved test surfaces.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeGrove","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-06T05:08:35.912359181Z","created_by":"JadeGrove","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:33.648810188Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:33.648810188Z","close_reason":"Focused remote rch clippy confirmed src/stream/fuse.rs is already clean on the current tree; no code change required","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:33.648789890Z","deleted_by":"system","delete_reason":"merge deletion","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","raptorq","stream","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:08:35.912359181Z","created_by":"JadeGrove","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2385,"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.4","author":"JadeGrove","text":"[2026-03-05 JadeGrove] Focused remote validation shows the src/stream/fuse.rs clippy frontier is stale on the current tree. Command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-jadeg-fuse-lib-clippy cargo clippy -p asupersync --lib --message-format=short -- -D warnings. Result: PASS on worker vmi1152480. No code change needed in fuse.rs; broad-gate blockers have moved elsewhere.","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:11:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-36m6p.5","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Clear current rustfmt drift in stream peekable combinator","description":"Narrow support slice for the current broad-gate frontier: normalize rustfmt drift in src/stream/peekable.rs only, without changing behavior or overlapping active stream test / fuse reservations. Goal is to remove one remaining formatting blocker so valid remote gate evidence can be re-established by the owning Track-E agents.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OliveCove","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-03-06T05:12:00.314145451Z","created_by":"OliveCove","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:23.238984622Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:23.238984622Z","close_reason":"Current tree already rustfmt-clean on src/stream/peekable.rs; earlier fmt frontier was stale","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-03-23T07:08:23.238965456Z","deleted_by":"system","delete_reason":"merge deletion","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-36m6p.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-36m6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:12:00.314145451Z","created_by":"OliveCove","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-agfwj4.1","title":"[audit][lab-oracle] Fresh-eyes audit of task/region/deadline invariants","description":"Audit unaudited lab oracle files src/lab/oracle/task_leak.rs, src/lab/oracle/region_tree.rs, and src/lab/oracle/deadline_monotone.rs. Build a precise mental model of their event-driven invariant checks, look for correctness gaps around reordering, repeated events, stale state, and deterministic violation selection, then patch and validate via rch-backed cargo commands if a real defect is found. Update audit_index.jsonl with the audited files and verdicts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SandyFrog","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:43:36.800509470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:57:44.384351671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:57:44.384006881Z","close_reason":"Completed oracle audit/fix slice; full rch cargo signoff blocked by unrelated src/messaging/redis.rs dead-code failure.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-agfwj4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-agfwj4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:43:36.800509470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3006,"issue_id":"asupersync-agfwj4.1","author":"SandyFrog","text":"Fresh-eyes oracle audit complete. Confirmed region_tree remains sound. Fixed two correctness gaps: (1) TaskLeakOracle now records/merges post-close task spawns as leaks, and (2) DeadlineMonotoneOracle now re-checks existing children when a parent deadline changes through on_budget_update(). Appended audit_index entries for all three files. rch-backed validation was attempted, but full signoff is currently blocked by unrelated dead-code failures in src/messaging/redis.rs; I also interrupted one rch fail-open local fallback after a worker sync error to stay within repo policy.","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:57:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-agfwj4.2","title":"[audit][peer-review] Deep review of recent agent-authored cancellation/network/runtime fixes","description":"Review recent agent-authored fixes across high-risk runtime surfaces (Redis pool/protocol cancellation, WebSocket close-handshake sequencing, timer-driver-bound sleep/shutdown paths, and adjacent recently changed files). Diagnose first-principles root causes, patch confirmed defects without reverting unrelated work, and validate with rch-backed checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMouse","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:55:50.755476088Z","created_by":"SunnyMouse","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:16:01.594894020Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:16:01.594541946Z","close_reason":"Peer review completed: fixed Redis cancellation-safety gap; remote cargo check green; remaining targeted lib test rerun blocked by rch local fallback.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-agfwj4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-agfwj4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:55:50.755476088Z","created_by":"SunnyMouse","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3014,"issue_id":"asupersync-agfwj4.2","author":"SunnyMouse","text":"Confirmed and fixed a cancellation-safety gap in src/messaging/redis.rs. The existing DiscardOnDropGuard only protected pipeline exec; pooled single-command exchanges and MULTI/EXEC transaction paths could still leave a protocol-desynced connection reusable after future drop. Extended discard-on-drop handling across cmd_bytes plus transaction begin/queue/exec/discard, added a regression covering a dropped queued transaction command, and re-ran rustfmt/git diff checks. Validation: rch cargo check --all-targets passed remotely; targeted lib test rerun was blocked because rch failed open to a local compile after one stale-worker attempt, so I killed the local compile rather than violate the offload rule.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:16:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-agfwj4.3","title":"[audit][random] Fresh-eyes archaeology of a random low-conflict subsystem","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftBluff","created_at":"2026-04-10T02:43:13.245515345Z","created_by":"SwiftBluff","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:19:51.215250809Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T03:19:51.214886943Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-agfwj4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-agfwj4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-10T02:43:13.245515345Z","created_by":"SwiftBluff","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3115,"issue_id":"asupersync-agfwj4.3","author":"SwiftBluff","text":"Random low-conflict slice selected from a shuffled file sample: tracing the trace-boundary subsystem via src/trace/mod.rs and src/trace/boundary.rs, then auditing the workflow with fresh eyes through callers/callees before patching any concrete defects found.","created_at":"2026-04-10T02:43:31Z"},{"id":3118,"issue_id":"asupersync-agfwj4.3","author":"SwiftBluff","text":"Fresh-eyes audit found a real topology-scoring bug: TopologyExplorer reduced only ∂2, which cannot represent H1 births or infinite H1 classes. Fixed by adding full-filtration boundary construction plus H1-only persistence extraction in src/trace/boundary.rs, then routing explorer scoring through that path. Added regression coverage for an unfilled triangle trace that previously scored as empty; remote rch test for topology_scoring_detects_unfilled_triangle_h1_class passed. Longer remote validation is still draining.","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:08:03Z"}]}

- Fixed a real bug in src/process.rs: spawn-time stdio setup failures killed the child but did not reap it, which could still leak a zombie on Unix. Added cleanup_child_after_spawn_setup_failure() and a direct regression test.
- Audited src/net/sys/windows.rs: no concrete correctness bug found in this slice.

 {"id":"asupersync-ah3v","title":"Integrate metrics with runtime and create examples","description":"## Overview

Integrate the MetricsProvider into the runtime and create examples showing Prometheus export and Grafana dashboards.

## Runtime Integration

### RuntimeBuilder
```rust
impl RuntimeBuilder {
    /// Set the metrics provider for the runtime.
    /// 
    /// # Example
    /// ```
    /// let runtime = RuntimeBuilder::new()
    ///     .metrics(OtelMetrics::new(meter))
    ///     .build()?;
    /// ```
    pub fn metrics<M: MetricsProvider>(mut self, provider: M) -> Self {
        self.metrics_provider = Some(Box::new(provider));
        self
    }
}
```

### Scheduler Integration
```rust
impl Scheduler {
    fn spawn_task(&mut self, region_id: RegionId, future: BoxFuture) -> TaskId {
        let task_id = self.next_task_id();
        // ... create task ...
        
        self.metrics.task_spawned(region_id, task_id);
        
        task_id
    }
    
    fn complete_task(&mut self, task_id: TaskId, outcome: Outcome<()>) {
        let duration = self.tasks[task_id].started_at.elapsed();
        self.metrics.task_completed(task_id, outcome.into(), duration);
        // ... cleanup ...
    }
}
```

## Prometheus Example

```rust
// examples/prometheus_metrics.rs

use asupersync::{RuntimeBuilder, metrics::OtelMetrics};
use opentelemetry_prometheus::exporter;
use prometheus::TextEncoder;

fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // Set up Prometheus registry
    let registry = prometheus::Registry::new();
    
    // Create OTel exporter
    let exporter = exporter()
        .with_registry(registry.clone())
        .build()?;
    
    let provider = opentelemetry_sdk::metrics::MeterProvider::builder()
        .with_reader(exporter)
        .build();
    
    // Create metrics
    let meter = provider.meter("asupersync");
    let metrics = OtelMetrics::new(meter);
    
    // Build runtime with metrics
    let runtime = RuntimeBuilder::new()
        .metrics(metrics)
        .build()?;
    
    // Run some workload
    runtime.block_on(async {
        // ... your async code ...
    });
    
    // Export metrics
    let encoder = TextEncoder::new();
    let metrics = encoder.encode_to_string(&registry.gather())?;
    println!("{}", metrics);
    
    Ok(())
}
```

## Grafana Dashboard

Create a JSON dashboard template at `examples/grafana_dashboard.json`:

```json
{
  "title": "Asupersync Runtime Metrics",
  "panels": [
    {
      "title": "Active Tasks",
      "type": "stat",
      "targets": [{
        "expr": "asupersync_tasks_active"
      }]
    },
    {
      "title": "Task Completions by Outcome",
      "type": "timeseries",
      "targets": [{
        "expr": "rate(asupersync_tasks_completed_total[5m])",
        "legendFormat": "{{outcome}}"
      }]
    },
    {
      "title": "Task Duration (p95)",
      "type": "timeseries",
      "targets": [{
        "expr": "histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(asupersync_task_duration_bucket[5m]))"
      }]
    },
    {
      "title": "Cancellation Rate",
      "type": "timeseries",
      "targets": [{
        "expr": "rate(asupersync_cancellations_total[5m])",
        "legendFormat": "{{kind}}"
      }]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Documentation

### Metrics Reference
Document each metric:
- Name
- Type (counter, gauge, histogram)
- Labels
- Description
- When it is updated

### Setup Guide
1. Add feature flag
2. Initialize OTel provider
3. Create OtelMetrics
4. Pass to RuntimeBuilder
5. Export metrics (Prometheus, OTLP, etc.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RuntimeBuilder::metrics() method
- [ ] Scheduler calls metrics hooks at appropriate points
- [ ] Prometheus example in examples/
- [ ] Grafana dashboard JSON
- [ ] Metrics reference documentation
- [ ] Setup guide in README or docs

 {"id":"asupersync-ai7u","title":"Implement OpenTelemetry metrics provider","description":"## Overview

Implement the OtelMetrics struct that exports asupersync metrics via OpenTelemetry to any supported backend.

## Dependencies

```toml
[dependencies.opentelemetry]
version = "0.21"
optional = true

[dependencies.opentelemetry_sdk]
version = "0.21"
optional = true
features = ["rt-tokio"]

[features]
metrics = ["dep:opentelemetry", "dep:opentelemetry_sdk"]
```

## OtelMetrics Implementation

```rust
use opentelemetry::{
    metrics::{Counter, Histogram, Meter, ObservableGauge},
    KeyValue,
};

/// OpenTelemetry metrics provider for asupersync.
/// 
/// # Example
/// ```
/// use opentelemetry::global;
/// use opentelemetry_prometheus::exporter;
/// 
/// // Set up Prometheus exporter
/// let registry = prometheus::Registry::new();
/// let exporter = exporter().with_registry(registry.clone()).build()?;
/// let provider = opentelemetry_sdk::metrics::MeterProvider::builder()
///     .with_reader(exporter)
///     .build();
/// global::set_meter_provider(provider);
/// 
/// // Create metrics
/// let metrics = OtelMetrics::new(global::meter("asupersync"));
/// 
/// // Use with runtime
/// RuntimeBuilder::new()
///     .metrics(metrics)
///     .build()
/// ```
pub struct OtelMetrics {
    // Task metrics
    tasks_active: ObservableGauge<u64>,
    tasks_spawned: Counter<u64>,
    task_duration: Histogram<f64>,
    
    // Region metrics
    regions_active: ObservableGauge<u64>,
    regions_created: Counter<u64>,
    region_lifetime: Histogram<f64>,
    
    // Cancellation metrics
    cancellations: Counter<u64>,
    drain_duration: Histogram<f64>,
    
    // Obligation metrics
    obligations_active: ObservableGauge<u64>,
    obligations_leaked: Counter<u64>,
    
    // Scheduler metrics
    scheduler_poll_time: Histogram<f64>,
    
    // Internal state for gauges
    state: Arc<MetricsState>,
}

struct MetricsState {
    active_tasks: AtomicU64,
    active_regions: AtomicU64,
    active_obligations: AtomicU64,
}
```

## Metric Initialization

```rust
impl OtelMetrics {
    pub fn new(meter: Meter) -> Self {
        let state = Arc::new(MetricsState::default());
        let state_clone = state.clone();
        
        Self {
            tasks_active: meter
                .u64_observable_gauge("asupersync.tasks.active")
                .with_description("Currently running tasks")
                .with_callback(move |observer| {
                    observer.observe(state_clone.active_tasks.load(Ordering::Relaxed), &[]);
                })
                .init(),
            
            tasks_spawned: meter
                .u64_counter("asupersync.tasks.spawned")
                .with_description("Total tasks spawned")
                .init(),
            
            task_duration: meter
                .f64_histogram("asupersync.task.duration")
                .with_description("Task execution duration in seconds")
                .with_unit(Unit::new("s"))
                .init(),
            
            // ... initialize other metrics
            
            state,
        }
    }
}
```

## MetricsProvider Implementation

```rust
impl MetricsProvider for OtelMetrics {
    fn task_spawned(&self, region_id: RegionId, task_id: TaskId) {
        self.state.active_tasks.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        self.tasks_spawned.add(1, &[
            KeyValue::new("region", region_id.to_string()),
        ]);
    }
    
    fn task_completed(&self, task_id: TaskId, outcome: OutcomeKind, duration: Duration) {
        self.state.active_tasks.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        self.task_duration.record(duration.as_secs_f64(), &[
            KeyValue::new("outcome", outcome.as_str()),
        ]);
    }
    
    fn cancellation_requested(&self, region_id: RegionId, kind: CancelKind) {
        self.cancellations.add(1, &[
            KeyValue::new("kind", kind.as_str()),
        ]);
    }
    
    // ... implement other methods
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] OtelMetrics struct with all metrics
- [ ] Feature-gated behind "metrics" feature
- [ ] All MetricsProvider methods implemented
- [ ] Correct metric types (counter, gauge, histogram)
- [ ] Proper labels/attributes on metrics
- [ ] Documentation with setup example
- [ ] Integration test with in-memory exporter","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:15:21.009134363Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:01:42.682386214Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:01:42.682290624Z","close_reason":"Implemented OtelMetrics provider, feature gating, and in-memory exporter test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-ai7u","depends_on_id":"asupersync-9foz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:21Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-aj3vy","title":"[CONFORMANCE] Add cancellation protocol and race-drain conformance tests","notes":"7 cancellation conformance tests implemented in conformance/src/tests/cancellation.rs:

- CANCEL-001: Timeout cancels running task (side-effect observable via AtomicBool)
- CANCEL-002: Cleanup ordering on cancellation (started but not finished flags)
- CANCEL-003: Nested timeout composition (inner tighter than outer)
- CANCEL-004: Race loser drain (loser terminal flag unset after timeout)
- CANCEL-005: Timeout no-op on already-completed task
- CANCEL-006: Multiple timeouts resolve independently (3 different timeouts with timing verification)
- CANCEL-007: Cancellation propagates via resource drop (3 oneshot channels closed on sender drop)

All tests use pre-created sleep futures to avoid capturing &RT in async blocks.
RT: Sync bound added to all_tests() to satisfy Send requirement.
Compiles clean, clippy clean, fmt clean. +The handoff remains machine-checkable through four explicit fields on

+the handoff stays blocked, `attachment_status` must remain
